evidence.summary: Updater trust-bypass achieved via TOCTOU race on descriptor swap
gdb plus ltrace confirm cache disclosure, heap corruption, and descriptor reuse
strace: 12ms TOCTOU window between sig-check completion and fd consumption; debug_gdb_commands confirms descriptor swap lands in window
proof-construction: forged package executes inside trust boundary; FLAG{updater-owned} captured; validated on clean-state re-run

notes: Initial approach tunneled on corrupting the package signature. Multi-route analysis identified 3 alternative paths: (1) race the descriptor swap before verification, (2) corrupt the verification result in memory, (3) redirect the package path post-verification. Route 1 viable: strace timing showed a 12ms TOCTOU window between signature check completion and package fd consumption. debug_gdb_commands confirmed the descriptor swap lands within the window on 95% of runs. proof-construction assembled the full trigger and captured FLAG{updater-owned}.
fixture session: deep updater near-miss upgraded to pass — multi-route analysis found TOCTOU timing window, proof-construction captured flag
